description: Plasmon field enhancement and gradient force (reference values only)
revision: Removed DIET evaluation claim. Gradient force model does NOT evaluate DIET mechanism. DIET = Desorption Induced by Electronic Transitions (hot electron mechanism).

reference_values_only.note: Gradient force only. NOT a DIET evaluation.

limitations: DIET mechanism is hot-electron-induced desorption, not gradient force
Gans theory is an approximation; FDTD calculation required for accurate values
Gradient force vs desorption energy comparison does not constitute DIET evaluation
I0=1e10 W/m2 poses nanogap damage risk due to local fluence amplification
gans_model_critical_note: Gans 1915 theory is for isolated prolate spheroids. Au nanogap is a dimer structure. Gans exact solution gives ~735x at gap=1.5nm (gap-independent for R>>1), while (lambda/gap)^1.5 gives 12479x (17x overestimate). FDTD required for accurate enhancement values.
